repos/.name: repos/
repos/.purpose: The project's git repositories — one domain per repository; the only remote-bearing git units in the project.
repos/.goesIn: Git repositories under repos/<name>/, one per domain.
repos/.doesNot: Non-repository material; any remote attached outside repos/ (any folder MAY carry a local, remote-less git for versioning, but never a remote).

context/.name: context/
context/.convention: plain Markdown; OKF opt-in for architecture-okf/
context/.purpose: Processed, authored knowledge — specifications, distilled research, Markdown/PDF documents a memo draws on.
context/.goesIn: Processed, derived documents, organized under a per-topic sub-folder.
context/.doesNot: Raw, unprocessed feeds (those go to data/); a generated artifact treated as the source it presents.

.memo/.name: .memo/
.memo/.purpose: The memo system's footprint, generated by memo-init — memos and the shared stores they draw on.
.memo/.goesIn: Memos under memos/<NNN-slug>/ and the shared stores (_requirements/, _references/, chronic/, plans/, …).
.memo/.doesNot: Project code (that belongs in repos/); .memo/ never carries a git remote.

design/.name: design/
design/.purpose: The project's design system and visual sources.
design/.goesIn: design.md (the active convention), design variants, and .pen / visual sources — organized under per-topic sub-folders once there is more than one surface.
design/.doesNot: Captured view proofs (those live in proofs/); the project architecture, which is a separate concern.

.tmp/.name: .tmp/
.tmp/.purpose: Ephemeral scratch — throwaway working files no part of the project depends on.
.tmp/.goesIn: Transient, discardable working material: intermediate output, scratch copies, staging for a multi-step operation.
.tmp/.doesNot: The sole copy of anything that matters; durable knowledge; committed content (it is gitignored).

scripts/.name: scripts/
scripts/.purpose: Environment and health scripts — the project's bring-up, tear-down, health, and session-lifecycle entry points.
scripts/.goesIn: The lifecycle script family — dev.sh / staging.sh (bring-up), cleanup.sh (tear-down), health-check.sh (health), and the start-the-plane / land-the-plane session-lifecycle pair — each under a meaningful subfolder.
scripts/.doesNot: A flat pile of scripts at the scripts/ root; non-script material.

.workbench/.name: .workbench/
.workbench/.purpose: The manual project configuration the workbench core reads.
.workbench/.goesIn: The hand-authored declared files — config.json (per-repository status), folder-lints.json, registry.json, and command-sops.json.
.workbench/.doesNot: Auto-generated or silently overwritten configuration; an id / name / project-id field (the folder name is the project id).

.wiki/.name: .wiki/
.wiki/.purpose: LLM-generated project wiki, an OKF-conformant knowledge bundle; reserved default-on, present in every project.
.wiki/.goesIn: The generated wiki pages (OKF nodes + untyped links), including the index.md entry point published as "overview".
.wiki/.doesNot: A second, drifting copy of the architecture graph (the wiki points at context/architecture-okf/, never copies it); the chronicle / timeline.

.browser/.name: .browser/
.browser/.purpose: Browser-automation session, scripts, and output — present only when the project performs browser automation.
.browser/.goesIn: The captured session auth.json, reusable automation under scripts/, and produced output/.
.browser/.doesNot: Committed material — auth.json and output/ are local-only and never pushed; hardcoded credentials in scripts.

.trash/.name: .trash/
.trash/.purpose: The recoverable deletion target — the project-local folder removals route through instead of erasing.
.trash/.goesIn: Material being removed, moved here (timestamped) so it stays recoverable until a human empties it.
.trash/.doesNot: Scratch that was never knowledge (that is .tmp/, see [19-tmp.md](./19-tmp.md)); it is emptied only by a human, never automatically.

flowmcp/.name: flowmcp/
flowmcp/.purpose: FlowMCP authored content and produced output — the footprint of the FlowMCP custom folder. It holds the material a project keeps and produces with FlowMCP (for example graded schema exports and reports), not the global CLI installation.
flowmcp/.goesIn: Authored FlowMCP content and produced output — schema grading exports, reports, produced artifacts.
flowmcp/.doesNot: The global CLI installation (that lives in ~/.flowmcp/, untouched); the regenerable namespace-index cache (that is .flowmcp/, gitignored).

data/.name: data/
data/.purpose: Raw inputs — feeds and source material as ingested, before processing; the input side context/ is derived from.
data/.goesIn: Raw feeds, dumps, and source files exactly as they arrive, un-processed.
data/.doesNot: Processed or derived material (that is context/); cloned repositories (those are research/ or repos/).

proofs/.name: proofs/
proofs/.purpose: Proofs captured when a view changes — the evidence a visual change was verified.
proofs/.goesIn: Captured proof artifacts (screenshots, before/after captures) tied to a view change.
proofs/.doesNot: Application snapshots (those are snapshots/); durable authored knowledge (that is context/).

research/.name: research/
research/.purpose: Cloned foreign / research repositories the project reads but does not own — distinct from repos/ (own repos) and data/ (raw inputs).
research/.goesIn: Foreign clones studied or referenced (for example dune, spellbook), each someone else's repository.
research/.doesNot: The project's own repositories (those are repos/); raw feeds (those are data/).

snapshots/.name: snapshots/
snapshots/.purpose: Application snapshots — captured states of the running application.
snapshots/.goesIn: Snapshot artifacts of the application, captured for reference or comparison.
snapshots/.doesNot: View-change proofs (those are proofs/); raw inputs (those are data/).

.worktrees/.name: .worktrees/
.worktrees/.purpose: The one consistent location for git worktrees — generated machinery, gitignored, with mandatory cleanup.
.worktrees/.goesIn: Git worktrees of the project's repos/ repositories, one checkout per branch under .worktrees/.
.worktrees/.doesNot: Authored content; a permanent home for a checkout (a worktree MUST be removed via git worktree remove/prune when its work is done).

projects/.name: projects/
projects/.purpose: The workbench root's container of all projects — one directory per project, each with its own project-level structure.
projects/.goesIn: One directory per project, projects/<name>/, each following the project folder contract.
projects/.doesNot: Cross-project machinery (that is cli/, templates/, or the root context/); project code directly (that lives inside each project's repos/).
